rules.title: Credential Manager Accessed
rules.description: A process has read credentials in the Credential Manager. There will be many false positives so check if the Process ID (PID) is that of known malware on the system.
rules.author: Zach Mathis

rules.title: Credential Manager Enumerated
rules.description: A process has enumerated credential information in Credential Manager. There will be many false positives so check if the Process ID (PID) is that of known malware on the system.
rules.author: Zach Mathis

rules.title: Logon (RemoteInteractive (RDP)) *Creds in memory*
rules.description: Type 10 logons can be dangerous as the credentials (plaintext or hashed passwords) are stored in memory (lsass process) and can be stolen by tools like mimikatz.
rules.author: Zach Mathis

rules.title: Logon (CachedInteractive) *Creds in memory*
rules.description: Type 11 Interactive logons can be dangerous as the credentials (plaintext or hashed passwords) are stored in memory (lsass process) and can be stolen by tools like mimikatz.
rules.author: Zach Mathis

rules.title: Logon (CachedRemoteInteractive) *Creds in memory*
rules.description: Type 12 logons can be dangerous as the credentials (plaintext or hashed passwords) are stored in memory (lsass process) and can be stolen by tools like mimikatz.
rules.author: Zach Mathis

rules.title: Logon (CachedUnlock) *Creds in memory*
rules.description: Type 13 logons can be dangerous as the credentials (plaintext or hashed passwords) are stored in memory (lsass process) and can be stolen by tools like mimikatz.
rules.author: Zach Mathis

rules.title: Logon (Interactive) *Creds in memory*
rules.description: Type 2 Interactive logons can be dangerous as the credentials (plaintext or hashed passwords) are stored in memory (lsass process) and can be stolen by tools like mimikatz.
rules.author: Zach Mathis

rules.title: Logon (NewCredentials) *Creds in memory*
rules.description: Type 9 logons can be dangerous as the credentials (plaintext or hashed passwords) are stored in memory (lsass process) and can be stolen by tools like mimikatz.
rules.author: Zach Mathis

rules.title: Explicit Logon Attempt
rules.description:
(From ultimatewindowsecurity.com)
This log is generated when
1. A user connects to a server or runs a program locally using alternate credentials.
   For instance a user maps a drive to a server but specifies a different user's credentials or opens a shortcut under RunAs by shift-control-right-clicking on the shortcut,
   selecting Run as..., and then filling in a different user's credentials in the dialog box that appears.  Or a user logs on to a web site using new specific credentials.
2. When a process logs on as a different account such as when the Scheduled Tasks service starts a task as the specified user.
3. With User Account Control enabled, an end user runs a program requiring admin authority.  You will get this event where the process information is consent.exe.
   Unfortunately the Subject does not identify the end user.
4. Logging on interactively to a server with a domain account. (Two 4624 events will also be generated.)
This logon event is unique in that it is logged on the source computer and not the target computer like most other logon events.
Also, it will be logged regardless of the logon being successful or not so consider it an attempt and not necessarily an actual successful logon.
rules.author: Zach Mathis

rules.title: Explicit Logon Attempt (Noisy)
rules.description:
(From ultimatewindowsecurity.com)
This log is generated when
1. A user connects to a server or runs a program locally using alternate credentials.
   For instance a user maps a drive to a server but specifies a different user's credentials or opens a shortcut under RunAs by shift-control-right-clicking on the shortcut,
   selecting Run as..., and then filling in a different user's credentials in the dialog box that appears.  Or a user logs on to a web site using new specific credentials.
2. When a process logs on as a different account such as when the Scheduled Tasks service starts a task as the specified user.
3. With User Account Control enabled, an end user runs a program requiring admin authority.  You will get this event where the process information is consent.exe.
   Unfortunately the Subject does not identify the end user.
4. Logging on interactively to a server with a domain account. (Two 4624 events will also be generated.)
This logon event is unique in that it is logged on the source computer and not the target computer like most other logon events.
Also, it will be logged regardless of the logon being successful or not so consider it an attempt and not necessarily an actual successful logon.
rules.author: Zach Mathis

rules.title: PW Spray
rules.description: Search for many 4648 explicit credential logon attempts in a short period of time.
rules.author: Zach Mathis

rules.title: Explicit Logon Attempt (Susp Proc) - Possible Mimikatz PrivEsc
rules.description: Detects explicit credential logons with suspicous processes like powershell and wmic which are often abused by malware like Cobalt Strike or Mimikatz for user impersonation.
rules.author: Zach Mathis

rules.title: Proc Access (Sysmon Alert)
rules.description:
The process accessed event reports when a process opens another process, an operation that is often followed by information queries or reading and writing the address space of the target process. 
This enables detection of hacking tools that read the memory contents of processes like Local Security Authority (Lsass.exe) in order to steal credentials for use in Pass-the-Hash attacks. 
Enabling it can generate significant amounts of logging if there are diagnostic utilities active that repeatedly open processes to query their state, so it generally should only be done so with filters that remove expected accesses.
rules.author: Zach Mathis

rules.title: Proc Access
rules.description:
The process accessed event reports when a process opens another process, an operation that is often followed by information queries or reading and writing the address space of the target process. 
This enables detection of hacking tools that read the memory contents of processes like Local Security Authority (Lsass.exe) in order to steal credentials for use in Pass-the-Hash attacks. 
Enabling it can generate significant amounts of logging if there are diagnostic utilities active that repeatedly open processes to query their state, so it generally should only be done so with filters that remove expected accesses.
Source User and Target User are not available in earlier versions of Sysmon.
rules.author: Zach Mathis

rules.title: LSASS Process Crashed - Application
rules.description:
Detects Windows error reporting events where the process that crashed is LSASS (Local Security Authority Subsystem Service).
This could be the cause of a provoked crash by techniques such as Lsass-Shtinkering to dump credentials.
rules.author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Accessing Encrypted Credentials from Google Chrome Login Database
rules.description:
Adversaries may acquire credentials from web browsers by reading files specific to the target browser.
Web browsers commonly save credentials such as website usernames and passwords so that they do not need to be entered manually in the future.
Web browsers typically store the credentials in an encrypted format within a credential store.
rules.author: frack113

rules.title: Credential Acquisition via Registry Hive Dumping
rules.description: Detects Credential Acquisition via Registry Hive Dumping
rules.author: Tim Rauch

rules.title: Registry Dump of SAM Creds and Secrets
rules.description: Adversaries may attempt to extract credential material from the Security Account Manager (SAM) database either through Windows Registry where the SAM database is stored
rules.author: frack113

rules.title: Potential Russian APT Credential Theft Activity
rules.description: Detects Russian group activity as described in Global Threat Report 2019 by Crowdstrike
rules.author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Potential APT FIN7 Exploitation Activity
rules.description:
Detects potential APT FIN7 exploitation activity as reported by Google.
In order to obtain initial access, FIN7 used compromised Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) credentials to login to a target server and initiate specific Windows process chains.
rules.author: Alex Walston (@4ayymm)

rules.title: Commvault QLogin with PublicSharingUser and GUID Password (CVE-2025-57788)
rules.description:
Detects a qlogin.exe command attempting to authenticate as the internal `_+_PublicSharingUser_` using a GUID as the password.
This could be an indicator of an attacker exploiting CVE-2025-57788 to gain initial access using leaked credentials.
rules.author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Suspicious Get-ADDBAccount Usage
rules.description: Detects suspicious invocation of the Get-ADDBAccount script that reads from a ntds.dit file and may be used to get access to credentials without using any credential dumpers
rules.author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Access to Browser Login Data
rules.description:
Adversaries may acquire credentials from web browsers by reading files specific to the target browser.
Web browsers commonly save credentials such as website usernames and passwords so that they do not need to be entered manually in the future.
Web browsers typically store the credentials in an encrypted format within a credential store.
rules.author: frack113

rules.title: Create Volume Shadow Copy with Powershell
rules.description: Adversaries may attempt to access or create a copy of the Active Directory domain database in order to steal credential information
rules.author: frack113

rules.title: Dump Credentials from Windows Credential Manager With PowerShell
rules.description:
Adversaries may search for common password storage locations to obtain user credentials.
Passwords are stored in several places on a system, depending on the operating system or application holding the credentials.
rules.author: frack113

rules.title: Enumerate Credentials from Windows Credential Manager With PowerShell
rules.description:
Adversaries may search for common password storage locations to obtain user credentials.
Passwords are stored in several places on a system, depending on the operating system or application holding the credentials.
rules.author: frack113

rules.title: Powershell Keylogging
rules.description: Adversaries may log user keystrokes to intercept credentials as the user types them.
rules.author: frack113

rules.title: Powershell LocalAccount Manipulation
rules.description:
Adversaries may manipulate accounts to maintain access to victim systems.
Account manipulation may consist of any action that preserves adversary access to a compromised account, such as modifying credentials or permission groups
rules.author: frack113

rules.title: Potential Packet Capture Activity Via Start-NetEventSession - ScriptBlock
rules.description:
Detects the execution of powershell scripts with calls to the "Start-NetEventSession" cmdlet. Which allows an attacker to start event and packet capture for a network event session.
Adversaries may attempt to capture network to gather information over the course of an operation.
Data captured via this technique may include user credentials, especially those sent over an insecure, unencrypted protocol.
rules.author: frack113

rules.title: Potential Invoke-Mimikatz PowerShell Script
rules.description: Detects Invoke-Mimikatz PowerShell script and alike. Mimikatz is a credential dumper capable of obtaining plaintext Windows account logins and passwords.
rules.author: Tim Rauch, Elastic (idea)

rules.title: PowerShell Credential Prompt
rules.description: Detects PowerShell calling a credential prompt
rules.author: John Lambert (idea), Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Extracting Information with PowerShell
rules.description:
Adversaries may search local file systems and remote file shares for files containing insecurely stored credentials.
These can be files created by users to store their own credentials, shared credential stores for a group of individuals,
configuration files containing passwords for a system or service, or source code/binary files containing embedded passwords.
rules.author: frack113

rules.title: Suspicious Connection to Remote Account
rules.description:
Adversaries with no prior knowledge of legitimate credentials within the system or environment may guess passwords to attempt access to accounts.
Without knowledge of the password for an account, an adversary may opt to systematically guess the password using a repetitive or iterative mechanism
rules.author: frack113

rules.title: Remove Account From Domain Admin Group
rules.description:
Adversaries may interrupt availability of system and network resources by inhibiting access to accounts utilized by legitimate users.
Accounts may be deleted, locked, or manipulated (ex: changed credentials) to remove access to accounts.
rules.author: frack113

rules.title: Veeam Backup Servers Credential Dumping Script Execution
rules.description: Detects execution of a PowerShell script that contains calls to the "Veeam.Backup" class, in order to dump stored credentials.
rules.author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: New Generic Credentials Added Via Cmdkey.EXE
rules.description:
Detects usage of "cmdkey.exe" to add generic credentials.
As an example, this can be used before connecting to an RDP session via command line interface.
rules.author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Potential Reconnaissance For Cached Credentials Via Cmdkey.EXE
rules.description: Detects usage of cmdkey to look for cached credentials on the system
rules.author: jmallette, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Windows Credential Guard Registry Tampering Via CommandLine
rules.description:
Detects attempts to add, modify, or delete Windows Credential Guard related registry keys or values via command line tools such as Reg.exe or PowerShell.
Credential Guard uses virtualization-based security to isolate secrets so that only privileged system software can access them.
Adversaries may disable Credential Guard to gain access to sensitive credentials stored in the system, such as NTLM hashes and Kerberos tickets, which can be used for lateral movement and privilege escalation.
The rule matches suspicious command lines that target DeviceGuard or LSA registry paths and manipulate keys like EnableVirtualizationBasedSecurity, RequirePlatformSecurityFeatures, or LsaCfgFlags.
Such activity may indicate an attempt to disable or tamper with Credential Guard, potentially exposing sensitive credentials for misuse.
rules.author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: DeviceCredentialDeployment Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of DeviceCredentialDeployment to hide a process from view.
rules.author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Esentutl Gather Credentials
rules.description: Conti recommendation to its affiliates to use esentutl to access NTDS dumped file. Trickbot also uses this utilities to get MSEdge info via its module pwgrab.
rules.author: sam0x90

rules.title: Copying Sensitive Files with Credential Data
rules.description: Files with well-known filenames (sensitive files with credential data) copying
rules.author: Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community

rules.title: Github Self-Hosted Runner Execution
rules.description:
Detects GitHub self-hosted runners executing workflows on local infrastructure that could be abused for persistence and code execution.
Shai-Hulud is an npm supply chain worm targeting CI/CD environments.
It installs runners on compromised systems to maintain access after credential theft, leveraging their access to secrets and internal networks.
rules.author: Daniel Koifman (KoifSec)

rules.title: HackTool - CreateMiniDump Execution
rules.description: Detects the use of CreateMiniDump hack tool used to dump the LSASS process memory for credential extraction on the attacker's machine
rules.author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: HackTool - LaZagne Execution
rules.description:
Detects the execution of the LaZagne. A utility used to retrieve multiple types of passwords stored on a local computer.
LaZagne has been leveraged multiple times by threat actors in order to dump credentials.
rules.author: Nasreddine Bencherchali, Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: HackTool - NetExec Execution
rules.description:
Detects execution of the hacktool NetExec.
NetExec (formerly CrackMapExec) is a widely used post-exploitation tool designed for Active Directory penetration testing and network enumeration
In enterprise environments, the use of NetExec is considered suspicious or potentially malicious because it enables attackers to enumerate hosts, exploit network services, and move laterally across systems.
Threat actors and red teams commonly use NetExec to identify vulnerable systems, harvest credentials, and execute commands remotely.
rules.author: Chirag Damani

rules.title: HackTool - Pypykatz Credentials Dumping Activity
rules.description: Detects the usage of "pypykatz" to obtain stored credentials. Adversaries may attempt to extract credential material from the Security Account Manager (SAM) database through Windows registry where the SAM database is stored
rules.author: frack113

rules.title: HackTool - Windows Credential Editor (WCE) Execution
rules.description:
Detects the use of Windows Credential Editor (WCE), a popular post-exploitation tool used to extract plaintext passwords, hash, PIN code and Kerberos tickets from memory.
It is often used by threat actors for credential dumping and lateral movement within compromised networks.
rules.author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Attempts of Kerberos Coercion Via DNS SPN Spoofing
rules.description:
Detects the presence of "UWhRC....AAYBAAAA" pattern in command line.
The pattern "1UWhRCAAAAA..BAAAA" is a base64-encoded signature that corresponds to a marshaled CREDENTIAL_TARGET_INFORMATION structure.
Attackers can use this technique to coerce authentication from victim systems to attacker-controlled hosts.
It is one of the strong indicators of a Kerberos coercion attack, where adversaries manipulate DNS records
to spoof Service Principal Names (SPNs) and redirect authentication requests like in CVE-2025-33073.
If you see this pattern in the command line, it is likely an attempt to add spoofed Service Principal Names (SPNs) to DNS records,
or checking for the presence of such records through the `nslookup` command.
rules.author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: LSA PPL Protection Setting Modification via CommandLine
rules.description:
Detects modification of LSA PPL protection settings via CommandLine.
It may indicate an attempt to disable protection and enable credential dumping tools to access LSASS process memory.
rules.author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Potential Credential Dumping Via LSASS Process Clone
rules.description: Detects a suspicious LSASS process process clone that could be a sign of credential dumping activity
rules.author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Samir Bousseaden

rules.title: Harvesting Of Wifi Credentials Via Netsh.EXE
rules.description: Detect the harvesting of wifi credentials using netsh.exe
rules.author: Andreas Hunkeler (@Karneades), oscd.community

rules.title: Notepad Password Files Discovery
rules.description: Detects the execution of Notepad to open a file that has the string "password" which may indicate unauthorized access to credentials or suspicious activity.
rules.author: The DFIR Report

rules.title: Sensitive File Dump Via Print.EXE
rules.description: Detects the abuse of the Print.exe utility for credential harvesting which involves using Print.Exe to copy sensitive files such as ntds.dit, SAM, SECURITY, or SYSTEM from the Windows directory in order to extract credentials, locally or remotely.
rules.author: Ayush Anand (Securityinbits)

rules.title: PUA - Memory Dump Mount Via MemProcFS
rules.description:
Detects execution of MemProcFS a memory forensics tool with the '-device' parameter.
MemProcFS mounts physical memory as a virtual file system, allowing direct access to process memory and system structures.
Threat actors were seen abusing this utility to mount memory dumps and then extract sensitive information from processes like LSASS or extract registry hives to obtain credentials, LSA secrets, SAM data, and cached domain credentials.
MemProcFS usage that is not part of authorized forensic analysis should be treated as suspicious and warrants further investigation.
rules.author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Dropping Of Password Filter DLL
rules.description: Detects dropping of dll files in system32 that may be used to retrieve user credentials from LSASS
rules.author: Sreeman

rules.title: Enumeration for Credentials in Registry
rules.description:
Adversaries may search the Registry on compromised systems for insecurely stored credentials.
The Windows Registry stores configuration information that can be used by the system or other programs.
Adversaries may query the Registry looking for credentials and passwords that have been stored for use by other programs or services
rules.author: frack113

rules.title: RestrictedAdminMode Registry Value Tampering - ProcCreation
rules.description:
Detects changes to the "DisableRestrictedAdmin" registry value in order to disable or enable RestrictedAdmin mode.
RestrictedAdmin mode prevents the transmission of reusable credentials to the remote system to which you connect using Remote Desktop.
This prevents your credentials from being harvested during the initial connection process if the remote server has been compromise
rules.author: frack113

rules.title: Potential Configuration And Service Reconnaissance Via Reg.EXE
rules.description: Detects the usage of "reg.exe" in order to query reconnaissance information from the registry. Adversaries may interact with the Windows registry to gather information about credentials, the system, configuration, and installed software.
rules.author: Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community

rules.title: Enumeration for 3rd Party Creds From CLI
rules.description: Detects processes that query known 3rd party registry keys that holds credentials via commandline
rules.author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Registry Export of Third-Party Credentials
rules.description:
Detects the use of reg.exe to export registry paths associated with third-party credentials.
Credential stealers have been known to use this technique to extract sensitive information from the registry.
rules.author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Potential Credential Dumping Attempt Using New NetworkProvider - CLI
rules.description: Detects when an attacker tries to add a new network provider in order to dump clear text credentials, similar to how the NPPSpy tool does it
rules.author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Capture Credentials with Rpcping.exe
rules.description: Detects using Rpcping.exe to send a RPC test connection to the target server (-s) and force the NTLM hash to be sent in the process.
rules.author: Julia Fomina, oscd.community

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious Rundll32.EXE Execution of UDL File
rules.description:
Detects the execution of rundll32.exe with the oledb32.dll library to open a UDL file.
Threat actors can abuse this technique as a phishing vector to capture authentication credentials or other sensitive data.
rules.author: @kostastsale

rules.title: VeeamBackup Database Credentials Dump Via Sqlcmd.EXE
rules.description: Detects dump of credentials in VeeamBackup dbo
rules.author: frack113

rules.title: Potential Browser Data Stealing
rules.description:
Adversaries may acquire credentials from web browsers by reading files specific to the target browser.
Web browsers commonly save credentials such as website usernames and passwords so that they do not need to be entered manually in the future.
Web browsers typically store the credentials in an encrypted format within a credential store.
rules.author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Private Keys Reconnaissance Via CommandLine Tools
rules.description: Adversaries may search for private key certificate files on compromised systems for insecurely stored credential
rules.author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Script Interpreter Spawning Credential Scanner - Windows
rules.description:
Detects a script interpreter process (like node.js or bun) spawning a known credential scanning tool (e.g., trufflehog, gitleaks).
This behavior is indicative of an attempt to find and steal secrets, as seen in the "Shai-Hulud: The Second Coming" campaign.
rules.author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Shadow Copies Creation Using Operating Systems Utilities
rules.description: Shadow Copies creation using operating systems utilities, possible credential access
rules.author: Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community

rules.title: Potential LSASS Process Dump Via Procdump
rules.description:
Detects potential credential harvesting attempts through LSASS memory dumps using ProcDump.
This rule identifies suspicious command-line patterns that combine memory dump flags (-ma, -mm, -mp) with LSASS-related process markers.
LSASS (Local Security Authority Subsystem Service) contains sensitive authentication data including plaintext passwords, NTLM hashes, and Kerberos tickets in memory.
Attackers commonly dump LSASS memory to extract credentials for lateral movement and privilege escalation.
rules.author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Windows Credential Manager Access via VaultCmd
rules.description: List credentials currently stored in Windows Credential Manager via the native Windows utility vaultcmd.exe
rules.author: frack113

rules.title: Sensitive File Dump Via Wbadmin.EXE
rules.description:
Detects the dump of highly sensitive files such as "NTDS.DIT" and "SECURITY" hive.
Attackers can leverage the "wbadmin" utility in order to dump sensitive files that might contain credential or sensitive information.
rules.author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113

rules.title: File Recovery From Backup Via Wbadmin.EXE
rules.description:
Detects the recovery of files from backups via "wbadmin.exe".
Attackers can restore sensitive files such as NTDS.DIT or Registry Hives from backups in order to potentially extract credentials.
rules.author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113

rules.title: Sensitive File Recovery From Backup Via Wbadmin.EXE
rules.description:
Detects the dump of highly sensitive files such as "NTDS.DIT" and "SECURITY" hive.
Attackers can leverage the "wbadmin" utility in order to dump sensitive files that might contain credential or sensitive information.
rules.author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113

rules.title: Webshell Hacking Activity Patterns
rules.description: Detects certain parent child patterns found in cases in which a web shell is used to perform certain credential dumping or exfiltration activities on a compromised system
rules.author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Potential Credential Dumping Via WER
rules.description: Detects potential credential dumping via Windows Error Reporting LSASS Shtinkering technique which uses the Windows Error Reporting to dump lsass
rules.author: @pbssubhash , Nasreddine Bencherchali

rules.title: Wdigest CredGuard Registry Modification
rules.description:
Detects potential malicious modification of the property value of IsCredGuardEnabled from
HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\WDigest to disable Cred Guard on a system.
This is usually used with UseLogonCredential to manipulate the caching credentials.
rules.author: Roberto Rodriguez (Cyb3rWard0g), OTR (Open Threat Research)

rules.title: Windows Credential Editor Registry
rules.description: Detects the use of Windows Credential Editor (WCE)
rules.author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Potential Credential Dumping Via LSASS SilentProcessExit Technique
rules.description: Detects changes to the Registry in which a monitor program gets registered to dump the memory of the lsass.exe process
rules.author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Windows Credential Guard Disabled - Registry
rules.description:
Detects attempts to disable Windows Credential Guard by setting registry values to 0. Credential Guard uses virtualization-based security to isolate secrets so that only privileged system software can access them.
Adversaries may disable Credential Guard to gain access to sensitive credentials stored in the system, such as NTLM hashes and Kerberos tickets, which can be used for lateral movement and privilege escalation.
rules.author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: RestrictedAdminMode Registry Value Tampering
rules.description:
Detects changes to the "DisableRestrictedAdmin" registry value in order to disable or enable RestrictedAdmin mode.
RestrictedAdmin mode prevents the transmission of reusable credentials to the remote system to which you connect using Remote Desktop.
This prevents your credentials from being harvested during the initial connection process if the remote server has been compromise
rules.author: frack113

rules.title: Potential Credential Dumping Attempt Using New NetworkProvider - REG
rules.description: Detects when an attacker tries to add a new network provider in order to dump clear text credentials, similar to how the NPPSpy tool does it
rules.author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Wdigest Enable UseLogonCredential
rules.description: Detects potential malicious modification of the property value of UseLogonCredential from HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\WDigest to enable clear-text credentials
rules.author: Roberto Rodriguez (Cyb3rWard0g), OTR (Open Threat Research)

rules.title: Potential Access Token Abuse
rules.description: Detects potential token impersonation and theft. Example, when using "DuplicateToken(Ex)" and "ImpersonateLoggedOnUser" with the "LOGON32_LOGON_NEW_CREDENTIALS flag".
rules.author: Michaela Adams, Zach Mathis

rules.title: Successful Overpass the Hash Attempt
rules.description: Detects successful logon with logon type 9 (NewCredentials) which matches the Overpass the Hash behavior of e.g Mimikatz's sekurlsa::pth module.
rules.author: Roberto Rodriguez (source), Dominik Schaudel (rule)

rules.title: Outgoing Logon with New Credentials
rules.description: Detects logon events that specify new credentials
rules.author: Max Altgelt (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Active Directory Replication from Non Machine Account
rules.description: Detects potential abuse of Active Directory Replication Service (ADRS) from a non machine account to request credentials.
rules.author: Roberto Rodriguez @Cyb3rWard0g

rules.title: Active Directory User Backdoors
rules.description: Detects scenarios where one can control another users or computers account without having to use their credentials.
rules.author: @neu5ron

rules.title: Possible Impacket SecretDump Remote Activity
rules.description: Detect AD credential dumping using impacket secretdump HKTL
rules.author: Samir Bousseaden, wagga

rules.title: Potential Kerberos Coercion by Spoofing SPNs via DNS Manipulation
rules.description:
Detects modifications to DNS records in Active Directory where the Distinguished Name (DN) contains a base64-encoded blob
matching the pattern "1UWhRCAAAAA...BAAAA". This pattern corresponds to a marshaled CREDENTIAL_TARGET_INFORMATION structure,
commonly used in Kerberos coercion attacks. Adversaries may exploit this to coerce victim systems into authenticating to
attacker-controlled hosts by spoofing SPNs via DNS. It is one of the strong indicators of a Kerberos coercion attack,.
where adversaries manipulate DNS records to spoof Service Principal Names (SPNs) and redirect authentication requests like CVE-2025-33073.
Please investigate the user account that made the changes, as it is likely a low-privileged account that has been compromised.
rules.author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Credential Dumping Tools Service Execution - Security
rules.description: Detects well-known credential dumping tools execution via service execution events
rules.author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community

rules.title: File Access Of Signal Desktop Sensitive Data
rules.description:
Detects access to Signal Desktop's sensitive data files: db.sqlite and config.json.
The db.sqlite file in Signal Desktop stores all locally saved messages in an encrypted SQLite database, while the config.json contains the decryption key needed to access that data.
Since the key is stored in plain text, a threat actor who gains access to both files can decrypt and read sensitive messages without needing the users credentials.
Currently the rule only covers the default Signal installation path in AppData\Roaming. Signal Portable installations may use different paths based on user configuration. Additional paths can be added to the selection as needed.
rules.author: Andreas Braathen (mnemonic.io)

rules.title: Suspicious Remote Logon with Explicit Credentials
rules.description: Detects suspicious processes logging on with explicit credentials
rules.author: oscd.community, Teymur Kheirkhabarov @HeirhabarovT, Zach Stanford @svch0st, Tim Shelton

rules.title: Possible Shadow Credentials Added
rules.description: Detects possible addition of shadow credentials to an active directory object.
rules.author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Elastic (idea)

rules.title: Transferring Files with Credential Data via Network Shares
rules.description: Transferring files with well-known filenames (sensitive files with credential data) using network shares
rules.author: Teymur Kheirkhabarov, oscd.community

rules.title: Credential Dumping Tools Service Execution - System
rules.description: Detects well-known credential dumping tools execution via service execution events
rules.author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community

rules.title: Potential Registry Reconnaissance Via PowerShell Script
rules.description: Detects PowerShell scripts with potential registry reconnaissance capabilities. Adversaries may interact with the Windows registry to gather information about the system credentials, configuration, and installed software.
rules.author: frack113

rules.title: Access To Browser Credential Files By Uncommon Applications - Security
rules.description: Detects file access requests to browser credential stores by uncommon processes. Could indicate potential attempt of credential stealing This rule requires heavy baselining before usage.
rules.author: Daniel Koifman (@Koifsec), Nasreddine Bencherchali

rules.title: Stored Credentials in Fake Files
rules.description: Search for accessing of fake files with stored credentials
rules.author: Teymur Kheirkhabarov (idea), Ryan Plas (rule), oscd.community

rules.title: Malicious Service Installations
rules.description: Detects known malicious service installs that only appear in cases of lateral movement, credential dumping, and other suspicious activities.
rules.author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community (update)

rules.title: Password Spraying via Explicit Credentials
rules.description: Detects a single user failing to authenticate to multiple users using explicit credentials.
rules.author: Mauricio Velazco, Zach Mathis

rules.title: Remote Thread Creation In Mstsc.Exe From Suspicious Location
rules.description:
Detects remote thread creation in the "mstsc.exe" process by a process located in a potentially suspicious location.
This technique is often used by attackers in order to hook some APIs used by DLLs loaded by "mstsc.exe" during RDP authentications in order to steal credentials.
rules.author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Potential Credential Dumping Attempt Via PowerShell Remote Thread
rules.description: Detects remote thread creation by PowerShell processes into "lsass.exe"
rules.author: oscd.community, Natalia Shornikova

rules.title: Credential Dumping Tools Service Execution
rules.description: Detects well-known credential dumping tools execution via service execution events
rules.author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community

rules.title: Suspicious Unattend.xml File Access
rules.description:
Attempts to access unattend.xml, where credentials are commonly stored, within the Panther directory where installation logs are stored.
If these files exist, their contents will be displayed. They are used to store credentials/answers during the unattended windows install process
rules.author: frack113

rules.title: CreateMiniDump Hacktool
rules.description: Detects the use of CreateMiniDump hack tool used to dump the LSASS process memory for credential extraction on the attacker's machine
rules.author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Credential Dumping by LaZagne
rules.description: Detects LSASS process access by LaZagne for credential dumping.
rules.author: Bhabesh Raj, Jonhnathan Ribeiro

rules.title: Credential Dumping Tools Accessing LSASS Memory
rules.description: Detects processes requesting access to LSASS memory via suspicious access masks. This is typical for credentials dumping tools
rules.author: Florian Roth, Roberto Rodriguez, Dimitrios Slamaris, Mark Russinovich, Thomas Patzke, Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Sherif Eldeeb, James Dickenson, Aleksey Potapov, oscd.community

rules.title: Credential Dumping by Pypykatz
rules.description: Detects LSASS process access by pypykatz for credential dumping.
rules.author: Bhabesh Raj

rules.title: Credential Acquisition via Registry Hive Dumping
rules.description: Detects Credential Acquisition via Registry Hive Dumping
rules.author: Tim Rauch

rules.title: Registry Dump of SAM Creds and Secrets
rules.description: Adversaries may attempt to extract credential material from the Security Account Manager (SAM) database either through Windows Registry where the SAM database is stored
rules.author: frack113

rules.title: Accessing WinAPI in PowerShell for Credentials Dumping
rules.description: Detects Accessing to lsass.exe by Powershell
rules.author: oscd.community, Natalia Shornikova

rules.title: Suspicious DNS Query Indicating Kerberos Coercion via DNS Object SPN Spoofing
rules.description:
Detects DNS queries containing patterns associated with Kerberos coercion attacks via DNS object spoofing.
The pattern "1UWhRCAAAAA..BAAAA" is a base64-encoded signature that corresponds to a marshaled CREDENTIAL_TARGET_INFORMATION structure.
Attackers can use this technique to coerce authentication from victim systems to attacker-controlled hosts.
It is one of the strong indicators of a Kerberos coercion attack, where adversaries manipulate DNS records
to spoof Service Principal Names (SPNs) and redirect authentication requests like CVE-2025-33073.
rules.author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Potential Russian APT Credential Theft Activity
rules.description: Detects Russian group activity as described in Global Threat Report 2019 by Crowdstrike
rules.author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Potential APT FIN7 Exploitation Activity
rules.description:
Detects potential APT FIN7 exploitation activity as reported by Google.
In order to obtain initial access, FIN7 used compromised Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) credentials to login to a target server and initiate specific Windows process chains.
rules.author: Alex Walston (@4ayymm)

rules.title: Commvault QLogin with PublicSharingUser and GUID Password (CVE-2025-57788)
rules.description:
Detects a qlogin.exe command attempting to authenticate as the internal `_+_PublicSharingUser_` using a GUID as the password.
This could be an indicator of an attacker exploiting CVE-2025-57788 to gain initial access using leaked credentials.
rules.author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Cred Dump Tools Dropped Files
rules.description: Files with well-known filenames (parts of credential dump software or files produced by them) creation
rules.author: Teymur Kheirkhabarov, oscd.community

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious DMP/HDMP File Creation
rules.description: Detects the creation of a file with the ".dmp"/".hdmp" extension by a shell or scripting application such as "cmd", "powershell", etc. Often created by software during a crash. Memory dumps can sometimes contain sensitive information such as credentials. It's best to determine the source of the crash.
rules.author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: HackTool - NetExec File Indicators
rules.description:
Detects file creation events indicating NetExec (nxc.exe) execution on the local machine.
NetExec is a PyInstaller-bundled binary that extracts its embedded data files to a "_MEI<random>" directory
under the Temp folder upon execution. Files dropped under the "\nxc\" sub-directory of that
extraction path are unique to NetExec and serve as reliable on-disk indicators of execution.
NetExec (formerly CrackMapExec) is a widely used post-exploitation and lateral movement tool used for
Active Directory enumeration, credential harvesting, and remote code execution.
rules.author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: HackTool - Potential Remote Credential Dumping Activity Via CrackMapExec Or Impacket-Secretsdump
rules.description: Detects default filenames output from the execution of CrackMapExec and Impacket-secretsdump against an endpoint.
rules.author: SecurityAura

rules.title: LSASS Process Memory Dump Files
rules.description: Detects creation of files with names used by different memory dumping tools to create a memory dump of the LSASS process memory, which contains user credentials.
rules.author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: LSASS Process Dump Artefact In CrashDumps Folder
rules.description: Detects the presence of an LSASS dump file in the "CrashDumps" folder. This could be a sign of LSASS credential dumping. Techniques such as the LSASS Shtinkering have been seen abusing the Windows Error Reporting to dump said process.
rules.author: @pbssubhash

rules.title: WerFault LSASS Process Memory Dump
rules.description: Detects WerFault creating a dump file with a name that indicates that the dump file could be an LSASS process memory, which contains user credentials
rules.author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: CredUI.DLL Loaded By Uncommon Process
rules.description: Detects loading of "credui.dll" and related DLLs by an uncommon process. Attackers might leverage this DLL for potential use of "CredUIPromptForCredentials" or "CredUnPackAuthenticationBufferW".
rules.author: Roberto Rodriguez (Cyb3rWard0g), OTR (Open Threat Research)

rules.title: BaaUpdate.exe Suspicious DLL Load
rules.description:
Detects BitLocker Access Agent Update Utility (baaupdate.exe) loading DLLs from suspicious locations that are publicly writable which could indicate an attempt to lateral movement via BitLocker DCOM & COM Hijacking.
This technique abuses COM Classes configured as INTERACTIVE USER to spawn processes in the context of the logged-on user's session. Specifically, it targets the BDEUILauncher Class (CLSID ab93b6f1-be76-4185-a488-a9001b105b94)
which can launch BaaUpdate.exe, which is vulnerable to COM Hijacking when started with input parameters. This allows attackers to execute code in the user's context without needing to steal credentials or use additional techniques to compromise the account.
rules.author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Suspicious Loading of Dbgcore/Dbghelp DLLs from Uncommon Location
rules.description:
Detects loading of dbgcore.dll or dbghelp.dll from uncommon locations such as user directories.
These DLLs contain the MiniDumpWriteDump function, which can be abused for credential dumping purposes or in some cases for evading EDR/AV detection by suspending processes.
rules.author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: HackTool - Credential Dumping Tools Named Pipe Created
rules.description: Detects well-known credential dumping tools execution via specific named pipe creation
rules.author: Teymur Kheirkhabarov, oscd.community

rules.title: Potential Credential Dumping Activity Via LSASS
rules.description:
Detects process access requests to the LSASS process with specific call trace calls and access masks.
This behaviour is expressed by many credential dumping tools such as Mimikatz, NanoDump, Invoke-Mimikatz, Procdump and even the Taskmgr dumping feature.
rules.author: Samir Bousseaden, Michael Haag

rules.title: Credential Dumping Activity By Python Based Tool
rules.description: Detects LSASS process access for potential credential dumping by a Python-like tool such as LaZagne or Pypykatz.
rules.author: Bhabesh Raj, Jonhnathan Ribeiro

rules.title: Remote LSASS Process Access Through Windows Remote Management
rules.description: Detects remote access to the LSASS process via WinRM. This could be a sign of credential dumping from tools like mimikatz.
rules.author: Patryk Prauze - ING Tech

rules.title: Credential Dumping Attempt Via WerFault
rules.description: Detects process LSASS memory dump using Mimikatz, NanoDump, Invoke-Mimikatz, Procdump or Taskmgr based on the CallTrace pointing to ntdll.dll, dbghelp.dll or dbgcore.dll for win10, server2016 and up.
rules.author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Suspicious Process Access to LSASS with Dbgcore/Dbghelp DLLs
rules.description:
Detects suspicious process access to LSASS.exe from processes located in uncommon locations with dbgcore.dll or dbghelp.dll in the call trace.
These DLLs contain functions like MiniDumpWriteDump that can be abused for credential dumping purposes. While modern tools like Mimikatz have moved to using ntdll.dll,
dbgcore.dll and dbghelp.dll are still used by basic credential dumping utilities and legacy tools for LSASS memory access and process suspension techniques.
rules.author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Credential Dumping Attempt Via Svchost
rules.description: Detects when a process tries to access the memory of svchost to potentially dump credentials.
rules.author: Florent Labouyrie

rules.title: New Generic Credentials Added Via Cmdkey.EXE
rules.description:
Detects usage of "cmdkey.exe" to add generic credentials.
As an example, this can be used before connecting to an RDP session via command line interface.
rules.author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Potential Reconnaissance For Cached Credentials Via Cmdkey.EXE
rules.description: Detects usage of cmdkey to look for cached credentials on the system
rules.author: jmallette, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Windows Credential Guard Registry Tampering Via CommandLine
rules.description:
Detects attempts to add, modify, or delete Windows Credential Guard related registry keys or values via command line tools such as Reg.exe or PowerShell.
Credential Guard uses virtualization-based security to isolate secrets so that only privileged system software can access them.
Adversaries may disable Credential Guard to gain access to sensitive credentials stored in the system, such as NTLM hashes and Kerberos tickets, which can be used for lateral movement and privilege escalation.
The rule matches suspicious command lines that target DeviceGuard or LSA registry paths and manipulate keys like EnableVirtualizationBasedSecurity, RequirePlatformSecurityFeatures, or LsaCfgFlags.
Such activity may indicate an attempt to disable or tamper with Credential Guard, potentially exposing sensitive credentials for misuse.
rules.author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: DeviceCredentialDeployment Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of DeviceCredentialDeployment to hide a process from view.
rules.author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Esentutl Gather Credentials
rules.description: Conti recommendation to its affiliates to use esentutl to access NTDS dumped file. Trickbot also uses this utilities to get MSEdge info via its module pwgrab.
rules.author: sam0x90

rules.title: Copying Sensitive Files with Credential Data
rules.description: Files with well-known filenames (sensitive files with credential data) copying
rules.author: Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community

rules.title: Github Self-Hosted Runner Execution
rules.description:
Detects GitHub self-hosted runners executing workflows on local infrastructure that could be abused for persistence and code execution.
Shai-Hulud is an npm supply chain worm targeting CI/CD environments.
It installs runners on compromised systems to maintain access after credential theft, leveraging their access to secrets and internal networks.
rules.author: Daniel Koifman (KoifSec)

rules.title: HackTool - CreateMiniDump Execution
rules.description: Detects the use of CreateMiniDump hack tool used to dump the LSASS process memory for credential extraction on the attacker's machine
rules.author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: HackTool - LaZagne Execution
rules.description:
Detects the execution of the LaZagne. A utility used to retrieve multiple types of passwords stored on a local computer.
LaZagne has been leveraged multiple times by threat actors in order to dump credentials.
rules.author: Nasreddine Bencherchali, Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: HackTool - NetExec Execution
rules.description:
Detects execution of the hacktool NetExec.
NetExec (formerly CrackMapExec) is a widely used post-exploitation tool designed for Active Directory penetration testing and network enumeration
In enterprise environments, the use of NetExec is considered suspicious or potentially malicious because it enables attackers to enumerate hosts, exploit network services, and move laterally across systems.
Threat actors and red teams commonly use NetExec to identify vulnerable systems, harvest credentials, and execute commands remotely.
rules.author: Chirag Damani

rules.title: HackTool - Pypykatz Credentials Dumping Activity
rules.description: Detects the usage of "pypykatz" to obtain stored credentials. Adversaries may attempt to extract credential material from the Security Account Manager (SAM) database through Windows registry where the SAM database is stored
rules.author: frack113

rules.title: HackTool - Windows Credential Editor (WCE) Execution
rules.description:
Detects the use of Windows Credential Editor (WCE), a popular post-exploitation tool used to extract plaintext passwords, hash, PIN code and Kerberos tickets from memory.
It is often used by threat actors for credential dumping and lateral movement within compromised networks.
rules.author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Attempts of Kerberos Coercion Via DNS SPN Spoofing
rules.description:
Detects the presence of "UWhRC....AAYBAAAA" pattern in command line.
The pattern "1UWhRCAAAAA..BAAAA" is a base64-encoded signature that corresponds to a marshaled CREDENTIAL_TARGET_INFORMATION structure.
Attackers can use this technique to coerce authentication from victim systems to attacker-controlled hosts.
It is one of the strong indicators of a Kerberos coercion attack, where adversaries manipulate DNS records
to spoof Service Principal Names (SPNs) and redirect authentication requests like in CVE-2025-33073.
If you see this pattern in the command line, it is likely an attempt to add spoofed Service Principal Names (SPNs) to DNS records,
or checking for the presence of such records through the `nslookup` command.
rules.author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: LSA PPL Protection Setting Modification via CommandLine
rules.description:
Detects modification of LSA PPL protection settings via CommandLine.
It may indicate an attempt to disable protection and enable credential dumping tools to access LSASS process memory.
rules.author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Potential Credential Dumping Via LSASS Process Clone
rules.description: Detects a suspicious LSASS process process clone that could be a sign of credential dumping activity
rules.author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Samir Bousseaden

rules.title: Harvesting Of Wifi Credentials Via Netsh.EXE
rules.description: Detect the harvesting of wifi credentials using netsh.exe
rules.author: Andreas Hunkeler (@Karneades), oscd.community

rules.title: Notepad Password Files Discovery
rules.description: Detects the execution of Notepad to open a file that has the string "password" which may indicate unauthorized access to credentials or suspicious activity.
rules.author: The DFIR Report

rules.title: Sensitive File Dump Via Print.EXE
rules.description: Detects the abuse of the Print.exe utility for credential harvesting which involves using Print.Exe to copy sensitive files such as ntds.dit, SAM, SECURITY, or SYSTEM from the Windows directory in order to extract credentials, locally or remotely.
rules.author: Ayush Anand (Securityinbits)

rules.title: PUA - Memory Dump Mount Via MemProcFS
rules.description:
Detects execution of MemProcFS a memory forensics tool with the '-device' parameter.
MemProcFS mounts physical memory as a virtual file system, allowing direct access to process memory and system structures.
Threat actors were seen abusing this utility to mount memory dumps and then extract sensitive information from processes like LSASS or extract registry hives to obtain credentials, LSA secrets, SAM data, and cached domain credentials.
MemProcFS usage that is not part of authorized forensic analysis should be treated as suspicious and warrants further investigation.
rules.author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: PUA - Mouse Lock Execution
rules.description: In Kaspersky's 2020 Incident Response Analyst Report they listed legitimate tool "Mouse Lock" as being used for both credential access and collection in security incidents.
rules.author: Cian Heasley

rules.title: Dropping Of Password Filter DLL
rules.description: Detects dropping of dll files in system32 that may be used to retrieve user credentials from LSASS
rules.author: Sreeman

rules.title: Enumeration for Credentials in Registry
rules.description:
Adversaries may search the Registry on compromised systems for insecurely stored credentials.
The Windows Registry stores configuration information that can be used by the system or other programs.
Adversaries may query the Registry looking for credentials and passwords that have been stored for use by other programs or services
rules.author: frack113

rules.title: RestrictedAdminMode Registry Value Tampering - ProcCreation
rules.description:
Detects changes to the "DisableRestrictedAdmin" registry value in order to disable or enable RestrictedAdmin mode.
RestrictedAdmin mode prevents the transmission of reusable credentials to the remote system to which you connect using Remote Desktop.
This prevents your credentials from being harvested during the initial connection process if the remote server has been compromise
rules.author: frack113

rules.title: Potential Configuration And Service Reconnaissance Via Reg.EXE
rules.description: Detects the usage of "reg.exe" in order to query reconnaissance information from the registry. Adversaries may interact with the Windows registry to gather information about credentials, the system, configuration, and installed software.
rules.author: Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community

rules.title: Enumeration for 3rd Party Creds From CLI
rules.description: Detects processes that query known 3rd party registry keys that holds credentials via commandline
rules.author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Registry Export of Third-Party Credentials
rules.description:
Detects the use of reg.exe to export registry paths associated with third-party credentials.
Credential stealers have been known to use this technique to extract sensitive information from the registry.
rules.author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Potential Credential Dumping Attempt Using New NetworkProvider - CLI
rules.description: Detects when an attacker tries to add a new network provider in order to dump clear text credentials, similar to how the NPPSpy tool does it
rules.author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Capture Credentials with Rpcping.exe
rules.description: Detects using Rpcping.exe to send a RPC test connection to the target server (-s) and force the NTLM hash to be sent in the process.
rules.author: Julia Fomina, oscd.community

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious Rundll32.EXE Execution of UDL File
rules.description:
Detects the execution of rundll32.exe with the oledb32.dll library to open a UDL file.
Threat actors can abuse this technique as a phishing vector to capture authentication credentials or other sensitive data.
rules.author: @kostastsale

rules.title: VeeamBackup Database Credentials Dump Via Sqlcmd.EXE
rules.description: Detects dump of credentials in VeeamBackup dbo
rules.author: frack113

rules.title: Potential Browser Data Stealing
rules.description:
Adversaries may acquire credentials from web browsers by reading files specific to the target browser.
Web browsers commonly save credentials such as website usernames and passwords so that they do not need to be entered manually in the future.
Web browsers typically store the credentials in an encrypted format within a credential store.
rules.author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Private Keys Reconnaissance Via CommandLine Tools
rules.description: Adversaries may search for private key certificate files on compromised systems for insecurely stored credential
rules.author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Script Interpreter Spawning Credential Scanner - Windows
rules.description:
Detects a script interpreter process (like node.js or bun) spawning a known credential scanning tool (e.g., trufflehog, gitleaks).
This behavior is indicative of an attempt to find and steal secrets, as seen in the "Shai-Hulud: The Second Coming" campaign.
rules.author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Shadow Copies Creation Using Operating Systems Utilities
rules.description: Shadow Copies creation using operating systems utilities, possible credential access
rules.author: Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community

rules.title: Potential LSASS Process Dump Via Procdump
rules.description:
Detects potential credential harvesting attempts through LSASS memory dumps using ProcDump.
This rule identifies suspicious command-line patterns that combine memory dump flags (-ma, -mm, -mp) with LSASS-related process markers.
LSASS (Local Security Authority Subsystem Service) contains sensitive authentication data including plaintext passwords, NTLM hashes, and Kerberos tickets in memory.
Attackers commonly dump LSASS memory to extract credentials for lateral movement and privilege escalation.
rules.author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Windows Credential Manager Access via VaultCmd
rules.description: List credentials currently stored in Windows Credential Manager via the native Windows utility vaultcmd.exe
rules.author: frack113

rules.title: Sensitive File Dump Via Wbadmin.EXE
rules.description:
Detects the dump of highly sensitive files such as "NTDS.DIT" and "SECURITY" hive.
Attackers can leverage the "wbadmin" utility in order to dump sensitive files that might contain credential or sensitive information.
rules.author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113

rules.title: File Recovery From Backup Via Wbadmin.EXE
rules.description:
Detects the recovery of files from backups via "wbadmin.exe".
Attackers can restore sensitive files such as NTDS.DIT or Registry Hives from backups in order to potentially extract credentials.
rules.author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113

rules.title: Sensitive File Recovery From Backup Via Wbadmin.EXE
rules.description:
Detects the dump of highly sensitive files such as "NTDS.DIT" and "SECURITY" hive.
Attackers can leverage the "wbadmin" utility in order to dump sensitive files that might contain credential or sensitive information.
rules.author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113

rules.title: Webshell Hacking Activity Patterns
rules.description: Detects certain parent child patterns found in cases in which a web shell is used to perform certain credential dumping or exfiltration activities on a compromised system
rules.author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Potential Credential Dumping Via WER
rules.description: Detects potential credential dumping via Windows Error Reporting LSASS Shtinkering technique which uses the Windows Error Reporting to dump lsass
rules.author: @pbssubhash , Nasreddine Bencherchali

rules.title: Windows Credential Guard Related Registry Value Deleted - Registry
rules.description:
Detects attempts to disable Windows Credential Guard by deleting registry values. Credential Guard uses virtualization-based security to isolate secrets so that only privileged system software can access them.
Adversaries may disable Credential Guard to gain access to sensitive credentials stored in the system, such as NTLM hashes and Kerberos tickets, which can be used for lateral movement and privilege escalation.
rules.author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Wdigest CredGuard Registry Modification
rules.description:
Detects potential malicious modification of the property value of IsCredGuardEnabled from
HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\WDigest to disable Cred Guard on a system.
This is usually used with UseLogonCredential to manipulate the caching credentials.
rules.author: Roberto Rodriguez (Cyb3rWard0g), OTR (Open Threat Research)

rules.title: Windows Credential Editor Registry
rules.description: Detects the use of Windows Credential Editor (WCE)
rules.author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Potential Credential Dumping Via LSASS SilentProcessExit Technique
rules.description: Detects changes to the Registry in which a monitor program gets registered to dump the memory of the lsass.exe process
rules.author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Windows Credential Guard Disabled - Registry
rules.description:
Detects attempts to disable Windows Credential Guard by setting registry values to 0. Credential Guard uses virtualization-based security to isolate secrets so that only privileged system software can access them.
Adversaries may disable Credential Guard to gain access to sensitive credentials stored in the system, such as NTLM hashes and Kerberos tickets, which can be used for lateral movement and privilege escalation.
rules.author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: RestrictedAdminMode Registry Value Tampering
rules.description:
Detects changes to the "DisableRestrictedAdmin" registry value in order to disable or enable RestrictedAdmin mode.
RestrictedAdmin mode prevents the transmission of reusable credentials to the remote system to which you connect using Remote Desktop.
This prevents your credentials from being harvested during the initial connection process if the remote server has been compromise
rules.author: frack113

rules.title: Potential Credential Dumping Attempt Using New NetworkProvider - REG
rules.description: Detects when an attacker tries to add a new network provider in order to dump clear text credentials, similar to how the NPPSpy tool does it
rules.author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Wdigest Enable UseLogonCredential
rules.description: Detects potential malicious modification of the property value of UseLogonCredential from HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\WDigest to enable clear-text credentials
rules.author: Roberto Rodriguez (Cyb3rWard0g), OTR (Open Threat Research)

rules.title: DMP/HDMP File Creation
rules.description: Detects the creation of a file with the ".dmp"/".hdmp" extension. Often created by software during a crash. Memory dumps can sometimes contain sensitive information such as credentials. It's best to determine the source of the crash.
rules.author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

rules.title: Potential Credential Dumping Attempt Via PowerShell
rules.description: Detects a PowerShell process requesting access to "lsass.exe", which can be indicative of potential credential dumping attempts
rules.author: oscd.community, Natalia Shornikova
